source_text: COUSINS, Rudolph Tarchous.—b. 1914; ed. Retreat Elem. Sch., Tutorial Coll., Lond. Sch. of Econ. (Lond. Univ.), dip. in soc-sci. and admin.; temp. clk., J'ca., 1932; 2nd cl. clk., 1938; 1st cl., 1943; co-op. offr., 1948; asst. registr., co-op. soc., 1950.

CREGAN, Kenneth Hamilton. — b. 1898; ed. Wesley Hall and Combermere Sch., Barb.; clk. and asst. acctnt., col. steamer serv., 1916; clk., col. sec.'s off., B. Guiana, 1942; asst. col. sec., 1946; sec. of various confs. and comtees., 1938–44.
CROSS, E. S.—b. 1905 ; ed. Eastleigh Sch., Hants. ; mil. serv., 1939 ; chargeman, G.C. rlys., 1938 ; progress offr., 1947 ; asst. wks. man., 1949 ; wks. man., 1956 (Ghana civil serv.).

De GRAFT-JOHNSON, G. K. B., M.B.E.—b. 1912; ed. Mfantsipim Sch., Achimota Coll.; mech. engr., G.C. rlys., 1937; asst. loco. supt., 1941; motive power supt., 1952; dep. chief mech. engr., 1953; chief mech. engr., 1954; dep. gen.-man., 1957; gen. man and hbrs. authy., 1960. (Ghana civil service.)

DOUGAL, J. K.—b. 1910 ; ed. Ardrossan Academy and Skerry's Coll. ; traffic inspr., S. Leone rly., 1940 ; asst. traffic supt., 1942 ; asst. traffic man., 1946 ; senr. traffic supt., G.C. rly., 1948 ; dep. traffic man., 1951 ; traffic man., 1952 ; dep. gen. man., 1955 (Ghana civil service).

DUNNILL, F.—b. 1920; ed. Queen Eliz. Gram. Sch., Barnet; naval serv., 1941–46; cler. offr., bd. of educ., 1937; exec. offr., min. of educ., 1946; higher exec. offr., 1948; asst. prin., gen. register office, 1949; prin., 1952; C.O., 1955; secon. D.T.C., 1961; trans. O.D.M., 1964; asst. sec., 1965.

EARLEY, A. H., O.B.E.—b. 1907; ed. Cirencester Gram. Sch. and Mercers' Sch.; mil. serv., 1939–45, col.; mem., econ. comsn. for Trieste and Venezia Giulia, 1945–46; chief cont. offr., cont. comsn., Germany, 1947; transport advr., 1952; chief ports manager, E.A.R. and H., 1954; secon., transport consultant to Inter. Bank Mission to Thailand, 1957–58; asst. gen. mangr., Tang., 1961. (Tanzania Govt. service.)

EGGLETON, H. W.—b. 1899 ; mil. serv., 1917–19 ; entd. C.A. off., 1920 ; asst. hd., gen. dept., 1942 ; appts. and passages dept., 1946 ; dep. hd., shipping dept., 1948 ; appts. and passages dept., 1949 ; gen. dept., 1950 ; appts. and passages dept., 1953 ; shipping dept., 1954.

EUSTACE, ROBERT WILLIAM BARRINGTON.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; capt., reserve of officers and late 4th Batt. S. Stafford Regt.; served in the S. African war with Canadian Mounted Rifles and A.S.C. (medal and six clasps); asst. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 10th May, 1904.

FOGARTY, J. M.—b. 1920; ed. Univ. Coll., Cork; mil. serv., 1942-46, lt.; asst. engr., Pal. rlys., 1946; E.A.R. & H., 1948; dist. engr., 1953; resdt. engr., S. Prov. rly. and port constrn., 1953; dist. engr., I, 1954; resdt. engr., W. Uga. ext. rly. constrn., 1955; asst. construction engr., 1957-62. (Uga. Govt. service.)
GARDNER, D. G. M., V.R.D.—b. 1914 ; ed. Monkton Combe Junr. Sch. and Oundle Sch. ; mil. serv., 1939–46 ; engnr. (roads), P.W.D., Ken., 1948 ; engnr., 1951 ; div. engnr., 1955
GARNER, W. R.—b. 1912; ed. Epworth Coll.; assessor, E.A. inc. tax dept., 1944; regl. comsnr., 1954; retrd., re-apptd., 1963.

GALLEY, R. A. E.—b. 1909 ; ed. Colfe's Gram. Sch. and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Tech. (Royal Coll. of Sci.) ; junr. tech. asst., fuel research stn., 1926-29 ; research offr., wool ind. research assoc., 1932 ; chmst., cl. II, dept. of war dept. chmst., 1934-37 ; exper. offr. (war time gr.) chem. insp. dept., min. supply, 1939 ; senr., 1942 ; prin. 1944 ; hd., res. and dev. dept., flax estab., 1945 ; senr. prin. sci. offr., agric. res. coun., 1946 ; secon., off of Lord Pres., 1951 ; dir., col. prod. res., C.O., 1953 ; mem., U.K. del., U.N. sci. conf. on conserv. and utilis. of resources and of W.H.O. expert comtee. on insecticides, 1949 ; working party on toxic substances in agric., 1950 ; poisons bd., H.O., 1951 ; misc. sci. and tech. pp.

GOCKING, W. E.—b. 1910 ; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., Univ. Coll., Lond. ; 4th cl. clk., customs and excise, Trin., 1931 ; 3rd cl., col. sec't, 1935 ; senr., 1937 ; prin. offr. sec't, 1940 ; secon. to central library servs., 1945 ; librarian, 1949 ; cent. library, Univ. Coll. of W.I., 1955 ; sec., various comtees. ; clk., leg. co., 1937–38.

GRAHAM-CUMMING, George, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liverp.), J.P. (1946).—b. 1906; ed. Royal High Sch., Edin., Hymers Coll., Hull (Lond. matric) and Edin. Univ. (C.P.H. (medal)); immig. offr. (Amoy Consulate), 1937, also M.O., Amoy Consulate and Kulangso; visiting M.O., C.H. and D., H.K., 1940; health offr. (E), 1945; (K), 1948; senr., 1950.
GRIEVE, D. D.—b. 1914 ; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, and Capetown Univ. ; asst. engnr., Ken., 1945 ; engnr., 1950 ; div. engnr., 1952 ; senr. engnr., 1955 ; D.P.W., Bech. Prot., 1956.

GROVE-WHITE, R. J.—b. 1916 ; ed. Rugby Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin. ; M.O., Mal. med. serv., 1939 ; interned, 1942–45 ; asst. to ag. prof. of medicine, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, 1946 ; T.B. specialist, S'pore, 1950 ; med. supt., Tan Tock Seng hosp., 1951 ; T.B. specialist, 1953.

GRINSTED, W. A., C.B.E. (1961).—b. 1904; ed. Annan Acad. and Royal Coll. of Science; war serv., 1943–46, sqdn. ldr.; prob., met. office (U.K.), 1920; meteorologist, B.E.A. met. serv., 1934; asst. dir., 1941; dir., Br. Caribbean met. serv., 1951; W. Indies met. serv., 1958–61; mem., Brit. polar year expedn., 1932–33; chmn., E. Caribbean hurricane comtee., 1952.

HORTON, Dick Crofton, D.S.C. (1945), Silver Star (American) (1943).—b. 1915 ; ed. Wykeham House Prep. Sch., Eastbourne Coll., and Queens' Coll., Camb., B.A., sci. ; on war serv., 1939–46, lieut. (R.N.V.R.) ; cadet, B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1938 ; dist. offr., 1938 ; trans. to Mal., 1946 ; asst. dist. offr., lower Perak, 1946 ; cl. IV ; cl. II ; asst. M.E.O., 1948 ; asst. sec., "A", S'pore, 1949.

HOWARD-DRAKE, J. T. A.—b. 1919; ed. Hele's Sch., Exeter; mil. serv., 1939–46, maj. (desps.); asst. inspr., min. of health, 1937; higher exec. offr., min. of nat. ins., 1947; asst. prin., C.O., 1947; p.s. to par. U.S. of S., 1949; prin., 1950; p.s. to S. of S., 1956. HOWE Yoon Chong.—b. 1923; ed. Gan Eng Seng English Sch., S'pore, St. Francis Instn., Malacca, and Raffles Coll., S'pore; teacher, Raffles Instn., S'pore, 1949; listener research offr., dept. of b'casting, 1949; estab. offr., 'D', 1951; mag., 1953; sec., public serv. comsn., 1955.

HUDSON, E. G.—b. 1915; ed. Trinity Gram. Sch., Wood Green and St. Bart's Hosp., London; mil. serv., 1940–46. R.A.M.C.; anaesthetist, Mal., 1947; senr. anaesthetist, S'pore, and lectr. in anaesthetics, Univ. of Mal., 1951.

HUNT-COOKE, A., O.B.E. (1958)—b. 1906; ed. Univ. Coll. Sch., Hampstead, and St. John's Coll., Camb.; educ. offr., Nig., 1928; senr., 1944; secon. Maur., 1942-47; advr., teacher training, Nig., 1954. (Nig. Govt. service.)

HUGHES, P., O.B.E. (1957)—b. 1912; ed. Bradfield Coll.; mil. serv., 1940–45, lt. R.N.; cadet, Swaz., 1931; p.s. to high comsnr., 1934; D.O., Basuto., 1936; Br. Sol. Is. Prot., 1947; sec., native affrs., high comsnr's off., W. Pac., 1953; dist. comsnr., Basuto., 1955; 1st asst. sec. (admin.), 1958; comsnr., local govt., 1959.

JACKSON, P. H. D., C.B.E. (1959), K.P.M. (1949).—b. 1910; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; prob. asst. comsnr., police, Mal., 1930; title changed to asst. supt., 1938; supt., 1948; asst. comsnr., 1951; dep. comsnr. (field), 1952–58.
JONES, D. D.—b. 1903 ; ed. Wallasey Higher Sch. and L'pool Tech. Coll. ; master mariner ; dep. marine supt., G.C. rly., 1939 (G.C. local serv.).
JOHNSON, R. J.—b. 1924; ed. Birmingham Univ. and Univ. S.A.; mil. serv., 1943–46, R.A.F.V.R., f/o; geologist, Uga., 1949; senr. geologist, 1959; prin. geol., 1960. (Uga. Govt. service.)

KELSHALL, A. K.—b. 1914; ed. Naparima Coll., San Fernando; 4th cl. clk., Trin., 1934; 2nd cl., 1939; 1st cl., 1943, senr. clk., 1947; prin. offr., cl. II, 1948; cl. I, 1949; asst. sec., 1952; estab. offr., 1956. (T'dad Govt. service.)

LACEY, G., C.I.E.—b. 1887 ; ed. Westminster City Sch., City and Guilds Cent. Tech. Coll. and Univ. of Lond. ; mil. serv., 1917–19, 1939–45, lt-col. ; asst. engnr., P.W.D., India, 1910 ; exec. engnr., 1918 ; under sec., U.P. govt., 1924 ; irrig. res. offr., 1928 ; suptg. engnr., 1934 ; ch. engnr., eastern canals, 1941 ; ret. 1942 ; prof., Thomason Coll., Roorkee, 1945 ; prin., 1945–46 ; advr., drainage and irrig., C.O., 1950–58 ; author of Regime Flow in Incoherent Alluvium (Cent. Bd. of Irrig., India, pub. No. 20), and other pp. on irrig.

LATULIPE, J. R.—b. 1914 ; ed. Royal Coll., Maur., King's Coll. and Univ. Coll., London ; extra teacher, Maur., 1939 ; asst. master, Royal Coll., 1945 ; educ. offr., gr. III, 1946 ; lectr., Coll. of Agric., 1950.

LEUNG, Fung Ki, M.B.E.—b. 1905 ; ed. Queen's Coll., H.K. Univ. ; asst. master, H.K., 1927 ; master (educ. offr.), 1946 ; senr. educ. offr., 1954 ; mem. of court, H. K. Univ. ; dir., widows' and orphans' pensions fund.

LIGHTFOOT-BOSTON, H. J., C.M.G. (1959).—b. 1898; ed. C.M.S. Gram. Sch., Fourah Bay Coll., S.L. and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; temp. police mag., S.L., 1946; police mag., 1948; senr. mag., 1955; puisne judge, 1957; ret., app. speaker, house of reps., 1957. (S.L. Govt. service.)

LOWE, G. C.—b. 1920 ; ed. Reigate Gram. Sch. ; mil. serv., 1941–46, flt. lieut. ; cler. offr., G.P.O., 1937 ; asst. audr., exchequer and audit dept., 1939 ; audr. (in absentia ), 1944 ; asst. prin., min. of civil aviation, 1947 ; prin., 1950 ; secon. C.O., 1954.

McCALLUM, D.—b. 1913; ed. Glasgow Univ.; mil. serv., 1942–46, flt. lieut.; meteorologist, air min., 1941; educ. offr., Ken., 1949; meteorologist, E.A. met. dept., 1952; regl. rep., Tang., 1956. (Tanzania Govt. service.)

McSHINE, Austen Hutton—b. 1906; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Port of Spain, and Loughborough Coll., Leics.; 2nd cl. clk., Trin., 1930; chief clk. and cashier, dist. admin., 1940; senr. clk., 1943; assessor, inland rev. dept., 1943; senr. inspr., 1946; assessor gr. I, 1949; supvr., 1950; asst. sec., 1953; asst. comsnr., inland rev. dept., 1954. (T'dad Govt. service.)

MATHER, R.—b. 1917 ; ed. Bolton County Gram. Sch. and Manchester Univ. ; mil. serv., 1943-46 ; temp. tech. offr., air min. met. off., 1939-46 ; prob. met. offr., S'pore, 1947 ; met. offr., 1949 ; asst. dir., Mal. met. serv., 1956 ; dir., 1957-59.

MILLAR, G. C.—b. 1922; ed. Primary Sch. and Crawford's Priv. Sch., Barbados, London and Oxford Univs.; asst. teacher, primary sch., Barb., 1941; asst. master, secondary sch., 1945; dist. inspr., schs., 1951; headmaster, gram. sch., St. V., 1956–60.

MILLER, H. C.—b. 1912; ed. J'ca Coll., I.C.T.A. and McGill Univ.; apptd. J'ca, 1934; senr. asst. master, J'ca Sch. of Agric., 1941; agric. offr., 1943; headmr., J'ca Sch. of Agric., 1948; chmn., Yallahs Valley land authy., 1951; dir. of agric., 1956; redesig. chief tech. offr., min. of agric. and lands, J'ca, 1957. (J'ca Govt. service.)

MILLS, D. O.—b. 1921; ed. J'ca Coll. and London Sch. of Econ.; 3rd cl. clk., treasury, J'ca, 1939; 2nd cl., 1943; 1st cl., 1949; statistician, 1950; senr., 1954; dep. dir., stats., 1957; prin. asst. sec., 1958. (J'ca Govt. service.)

MORRIS, O. H.—b. 1921; ed. King's Coll. Sch., Wimbledon and Balliol Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1941–45, capt.; asst. prin., C.O., 1946; prin., 1948; secon., S.L. admin., 1952–53; asst. sec., 1955; secon. D.T.C., 1962; trans. O.D.M., 1964.

MOUTOU, L. R., M.B.E. (1959).—b. 1911; ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; gr. II clk., Maur., 1933; chief off. supt., stat. off., 1945; spec. gr. clk., 1945; clk. asst., leg. co., 1949; clk., leg. co., 1952; prin. asst. sec., 1962.

NICHOLSON, E.—b. 1910; ed. Blackhall Mill Highertops Sch. and Rutherford Coll. of Technology, Newcastle; techn., gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; inspg. techn., 1952; asst. engnr., gr. I, 1955; sect. engnr., gr. II, 1957; gr. I, 1960; retd. and re-apptd., 1962; senr. asst. engnr., 1964.

NICHOLLS, K. V. W.—b. 1908 ; ed. Plumtree Sch. and Cape Town and London Univs. ; mil. serv., 1941–46, flt.-lt. ; S. Rhod. C.S., 1927–36 ; met. offr., air min., 1939 ; met., Mal. met. serv., 1947 ; E.A. met. dept., 1949 ; secon. Br. Caribbean met. serv., 1952 ; dep. dir., 1955 ; W. Indies met. serv., 1958 ; author The Finances of S. Rhod. during the first ten years of responsible govt. (1936) ; Some mathematical formulae (E.A. Met. Dept. Tech. Mem., 1952).

O'SHEA, C. P., O.B.E. (1963).—b. 1918; ed. Clonglowes Wood Coll., Ireland, and Prince of Wales Sch., Nairobi; learner gr. I, posts and tels. dept., Ken., 1937; asst. storekpr., 1938; acctnt.-gen's dept., 1939; clk. gr. I, 1945; dep. chief acctnt., P.W.D., 1948; chief acctnt., 1953; under sec.; dep. sec., 1960–64. (Ken. Govt. service.)

PEMBROKE, J. L., O.B.E. (1962).—b. 1912; ed. Univ. Sch.; asst. inspr., taxes, U.K., 1936; inspr., 1946; prin. admin. gr., 1950; asst. dep. comsnr., E.A. inc. tax, 1953; dep. comsnr., E.A. inc. tax dept., 1955; comsnr., 1962.
RABY, R. E.—b. 1911; ed. Bancroft Sch., Woodford Green; telecoms. engr., Nyas., 1951; senr., Fed. Govt., 1953–54; postmtr. gen., Nyas., 1963. (Malawi Govt. service.)

REED, N.—b. 1921; ed. East Grinstead County Sch.; mil. serv., 1943-47; Br. P.O., 1938-53; techn., gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1953; asst. engnr., gr. II, 1955; gr. I (old style), 1956; gr. I (new style), 1960; senr. asst. engnr., 1963.

REID, Major E. J. K.—b. 1905; ed. St. Augustine's Coll., Ashford; mil. serv., 1939-49, maj.; chargeman, G.C. rly., 1936; loco. foreman, 1941; asst. loco. supt., 1949; asst. wks. man., 1952 (Ghana civil service).
ROBERTS, I. L.—b. 1921; ed. Grove Pk. Sch., Wrexham; mil. serv., 1941–45; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Ken., 1946; engnr., 1950; div. engnr., 1956; chief hydraulic engnr., 1959–63. (Ken. Govt. service.)

ROLPH, R. L.—b. 1914; ed. Camb. and County High Sch. and Fitzwilliam Hse., Camb. Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-46, col.; planning engnr., P.W.D., Ken., 1950; dep. road engnr., 1954; asst. chief engnr. (admin.), E.A.R. & H., 1955; chief estab. offr., 1960; ch. admin. sec., 1962; ch. engnr., 1965.

ROUSSEAU, F. O.—b. 1905; ed. St. Aloysius Sch. for Boys, J'ca, and privately; 2nd cl. clk., audr.-gen's dept., J'ca, 1938; 1st cl., 1941; examr. of accts., 1945; senr. acctnt., posts and tels. dept., 1950; dep. P.M.G., 1954; P.M.G., 1960. (J'ca Govt. service.)

RUSSELL, David Alexander, A.M.I. Mech.E.—b. 1903 ; ed. Govan High Sch., Glasgow, Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow (dip. in engnrg. sci.), Glasgow Prov. Training Coll., higher nat. dip. in mech. engnrg., Scottish educ. dept., 1933-38 (teachers' certs.) ; ch. instruct., gov. trade sch., Haifa, Pal., 1938 ; prin., 1940 ; prin. asst. contrlr. of manpower, 1942 ; asst. dir., educ. (tech.), educ. dept., 1945 ; dir. of vocational training, 1947 ; prin., gov. tech. sch., G.C., 1948 ; ch. inspr., educ. (tech.), Nig., 1950.

SHANN, G. N.—b. 1918; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939–45, capt.; educ. offr., Tang., 1945; senr. educ. offr., 1958; author The educational development of the Chagga tribe ; jt. author Twende Tusome Sote .
SHIPPEN, J. F.—b. 1915; ed. Workington Secondary Sch. and L'pool Univ.; mil. serv., 1939–45; senr. asst. loco. supt., Mal. rlwy., 1946; works manager, 1954.

SINHA, A. C.—b. 1911; ed. Anglo-Chinese Sch., Raffles Inst. and King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore; student asst. surgeon, Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, 1927; asst. hse. surgeon, 1933; asst. M.O., 1935; hse. physician, gr. III, 1937; M.O. gr. II, 1945; gr. I, 1946; gynaecologist and obstetrician, 1951; part-time lectr., Univ. of Mal., 1956; del., 1st Asian congr. on obstetrics and gynaec., Tokyo, 1957.
SOULSBY, A. J. E.—b. 1911 ; ed. Bede Gram. Sch., Sunderland, and King's Coll., London ; educ. offr., Nig., 1944 ; prin., Edo Coll., 1951 ; asst. dir., educ., W. regn., 1954 ; dep. dir., 1956.
STORY, R.—b. 1912 ; mil. serv., 1943–45, sub.-lt. ; pilot, G.C. rlys., 1942 ; 2nd offr. (marine), K.U.R. and H., 1945 ; marine offr., 1946 ; comdr. (marine), E.A.R. and H., 1951.
TAIT, P. G.—b. 1914; ed. Edinburgh Academy and Peterhouse, Camb.; D.O., Ken., 1940; dist. comsnr., 19—; senr., 1960–65. (Ken. Govt. service.)

SWEANEY, W. D., C.M.G. (1965).—b. 1912; ed. Gillingham County Sch. and Lond. Sch. of Econ.; apptd. after compet. exam., cl. offr., inl. rev. dept., 1929; offr., customs and excise (compet. exam.), 1932; secon. C.O., 1943; prom. surveyor in absentia ; principal, C.O., 1948; p.s., min. of state, 1953; asst. sec., 1955; secon. D.T.C., 1961; trans., O.D.M., 1964.

TOUREAU, L. P.—b. 1913 ; ed. Royal Coll., Maur., and Middle Temple ; mil. serv., 1942-47, lieut. ; cler. asst., registr.-gen., Maur., 1933 ; gr. II clk., 1935 ; gr. I, P.W.D., 1949 ; dist. mag., 1952 ; civ. comsnr., 1955.

TRENCH, David Clive Crosby, M.C.—b. 1915 ; ed. Tonbridge Sch. and Jesus Coll., Camb., B.A. (Cantab.) : jt. serv. staff coll., 1949 ; on mil. serv., 1942–46, lt.-col. ; cadet, B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1938 ; dist. offr., 1940 ; seconded to W. Pac. H.C., 1941 ; seconded to B. serv. in Tonga, 1942 ; dist. comsnr., E. dist., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1944 ; ag. res. comsnr., periods, 1943–46 ; admin. offr., gr. I, 1946 ; first asst. sec., W. Pac. H.C., 1947 ; development sec., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1947 ; cadet offr., cl. II, H.K., 1950.

packets.flagged_value: E. district, B. Sol. Is. protectorate

source_text: VANDERPUMP, G. M.—b. 1920; ed. Beckford and Smith's High Sch., J'ca; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; 3rd cl. clk., sec't, J'ca, 1941; 2nd cl., 1945; asst. clk., gr. I, resdt. mag's court, 1947; dep. clk., courts, 1949; clk., courts, 1956; crown counsel, 1958. (J'ca Govt. service.)

VAN HEERDEN, C. J., O.B.E. (1958).—b. 1896 ; vet. offr., S.A., 1923 ; senr., 1930 ; sub-D.V.S., 1933 ; asst. D.V.S., 1937 ; under-sec., agric., 1944 ; vet. offr., Swaz., 1951 ; dir., land util., 1955.

WAINWRIGHT, R. G.—b. 1919; ed. King William's Coll. and Ramsey Gram. Sch.; mil. serv., 1940–46; Br. P.O., 1936–47; techn. gr. I, E.A. posts and tels dept., 1947; inspg. techn., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1953; asst. engnr., gr. I, 1955; senr. asst. engnr., 1956; senr. sect. engnr., 1962.
GRIEVE, THOMAS.—B. 1883; ed. Kirkcaldy and Edinburgh Univ.; asst. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., 1912; divnl. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., 1915; dep. ch. res. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., 1919; engnr. for constrn., 1922.

HELPS, GEORGE SYDNEY. — B. 1886 ; bldg. inspr., P.W.D., S.S. Mar., 1914 ; war serv., Oct., 1914-Nov., 1917 ; asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1918 ; supt., way and wks. depot, K. Lumpur, Dec., 1921 ; dist. engnr., grade II, F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1923 ; dist. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1927 ; snr. dist. engnr., do., 1936.

HUSSEY, JOHN DREW.—B. 1900; 2nd lieut., Devonshire Regt. & Tank Corps, Apr., 1918-May, 1919; pol. probr., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. asst. comandr. pol., various occasions, 1922-24; adjt., F.M.S. pol., Dec., 1925; A.C.P., various occasions, 1925-31; dep. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1933.
MAHON, WILLIAM GREAVES.—B. 1879; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1900; ch. clk., pol. mag. cts., dist. "A", 1919; pol. mag., dist. "E", 1919.

LENDRUM, SAMUEL JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. St. Luke, Cork, and pte. tuition; clk., Imp. mily. rlyrs., S. Africa, 1900; conf. clk., govt. comsnr.'s office, 1901; clk., Transvaal pol., 1902; ag. ch. clk., do. pol., 1905-8; supt. clk. do. pol., 1908; sub-inspr., S.A. pol., 1913; inspr. do. pol., 1918; snr. inspr., 1928; dep. comsnr., 1929; ch. dep. comsnr., 1933.

PETERKIN, EDMUND MURRAY.—B. 1886; agri. asst., dept. agr., Barbados, 1909; agri. instr., dept. sci. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1915; hort. supt., bot. gdns., 1917; clk. and sec., bd. agr., 1918; ag. asst. bot. and mycologist, 1922-23; agri. supt., 1928; investigated rice conditions, Br. Honduras, 1933.

WEILS, THOMAS EUSTACE, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1888; ed. Lincoln Cathedral Choir Schl.; statnmr., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1913; asst. traffic inspr., Tampin, Sept., 1915; wharf supt., Prai, Oct., 1916; asst. traffic man., July, 1918; asst. traffic man., July, 1926; senr. asst. traffic man., July, 1927; asst. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., Jan., 1932.
ASHLEY, Wilmot Sylvester. —b. 1904; ed. elem. sch.; apptd. constabulary, J'ca, 1924; asst. supt., police, 1948; recd. appreciation of S. of S. and Gov. in conn. with labour disturbances in 1946.

BADCOCK, William John, dip. agric. (Reading), N.D.A., A.I.C.T.A.—b. 1905; ed. Dunkeved Coll., Launceston, Reading and Camb. Univs., and I.C.T.A.; on mil. serv. 1940–43 and 1945–46, maj.; agric. offr., col. serv., 1931; seconded as senr. agric. offr., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1945; ch. soil conserv. offr., Nyasa., 1948; author of An Ecological Study on Sugar .

BESSON, William Willesbert, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Liverpool).—b. 1901; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., and Edin. and Liverpool Univs. (winner Is. schol., Trin.); gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, 1927; acted dist. comsnr. and mag., N.W. dist., 1935.

BERESFORD, Walter Marcus, M.C.—b. 1899 ; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin ; on mil. serv., 1916–19, capt. and 1939–41, maj. (desps.) ; police, J'ca., 1913 ; 3rd cl. inspr., J'ca. constab., 1920 ; asst. comsnr., police, Nig., 1924 ; comsnr., Cams., 1933 ; inspr., police (temp. attd.), J'ca. constab., 1943.
COLLEY, Peter.—b. 1907; ed. Downside Sch., Somerset; clk., Fiji, 1929; clk., res. comsnr., off., Br. Sol. Is. Prot., 1933; cadet, 1941; cadet, N. Heb., 1942; admin. offr., 1945.

COCHRAN, Harry Archibald, B.Sc., M.I.Min.E., M.I.M.M., M.Amer.I.M.E.—b. 1904; ed. Glasgow Acad., 1st cl. honss., and Univ. of Glasgow, B.Sc. (min., eng. with sp. distinc.), Dawson res. schol.; geol. engr., Nig., 1929; senr. geol. engr., 1937; senr. inspr. of mines, 1939; dep. ch. inspr., 1944; ch. inspr., 1947.

CORRY, Stuart.—b. 1900; ed. Switzerland and Germany, Cheltenham Coll., and Oxford Univ., M.A. (hon.) Oxon.; on mil. serv., 1941-45, capt.; aptd., 1923; cl. V, 1926; cl. IV, 1930; cl. III, 1935; asst. sec., govt. F.M.S., 1939; cl. II, 1940; cl. IB, ag. res. comsnr., Johore, 1947; ag. Br. advsr., Pahang, 1948; sec., comsn. of enq. into working of mines dept., 1940.
CRAWFURD, Berin Eustace.—b. 1904; ed. Eton Coll. and abroad; cadet, Br. Sol. Is. Prot., 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr., 1932; dist. offr., N. Rhod., 1936.

DANARAJ, Thamboo John, L.M.S. (S'pore.).—b. 1914 ; ed. King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore. ; hse. physician, gr. III, S.S., 1938 ; Mal., 1939 ; S.S., 1940 ; med. offr., S'pore., 1945 ; gr. I, 1946 ; tutor, coll. of med., 1947.

DAVY-HAYFORD, Mark, L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.C.P. & S. (Glasg.), D.T.M. & H. (Edin.)—b. 1902 ; ed. S.P.G. Gram. Sch., Cape Coast, Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Royal Infirmary, Glasgow ; junr. med. offr., G.C., 1937 ; med. offr., 1939.
